title: Chapter Forty-Seven: Encounter
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The morning of departure was clear and cold.

Yeon-shin received a travel pack containing provisions, various items, and travel expenses. As a blue warrior, he was responsible for everything -- logistics, route, the lives of the two white-levels riding with him.

Ma Jin stood at the Radiant Demon Wing gate. His scarred face worked through something difficult.

"See you later."

"The moment you complete the mission, we clash with the Hwangbo family. Let us meet then."

A silence. The scar at his mouth corner looked sheepish.

"...Missions can fail. Think of your safety first."

Yeon-shin knew those were not the words of a squad leader. He bowed silently to his uncle. Unlike his maternal grandfather Ma Yeon-jeok, he could not simply push away a man who felt genuine guilt and kept trying to reach across the gap between them.

"Don't overdo it either, squad leader. They say the Hwangbo family head is a counted master in Shandong."

"It is not sparring but war. We will attack together."

Yeon-shin shook his head, mounted the brown horse beside him, and flicked the reins. Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin fell in on either side. The Radiant Demon Wing seniors watched them go.

His first mission as a blue warrior.

---

Twenty days of hard riding, and nothing particular happened.

Just covering the Desolate Sect mark felt like entering a different world. The background that made people show respect or fear had vanished. He was nobody. A blue-clothed youth with a good sword and two companions.

*I have heard there is contempt that Desolate Sect masters experience in the martial world. This must be close to it.*

The Desolate Sect characters on their uniforms were covered with exquisite silk pieces. Official martial uniforms contained thread from the celestial Heaven Silkworm -- one strand at each vital point, embroidered vertically, buying brief moments against horizontal slashes. Worth more than iron plates, and far less conspicuous.

They changed horses at post stations. Galloped through the days. Shin So-bin rode well -- a direct descendant of a prestigious family, no hindrance at all. Hyeon Won-chang rode even better, grinning through the dust.

"Traveling through the martial world again. Always thrilling."

Yeon-shin did not answer. He was refining sword songs as they rode -- splitting true energy into dual strands inside the blade, controlling pitch, pushing toward the realm of sound techniques.

*The power of this martial art will ultimately depend on internal energy quantity. And I still cannot distinguish friend from foe.*

Problems for another day.

They stopped near Huizhou, under an unusually large ginkgo tree by the official road. Yeon-shin sat cross-legged, Northern Light across his knees. The Desolate Sword was not there -- left with Cheong Myeong to hide his affiliation.

*Woong.*

The blade sang. He split the energy finer, pressed it deeper.

*Goong!*

Louder. Clearer. A resonance he had never achieved before.

"Sword songs? It draws tremendous attention." Shin So-bin approached, black pupils sparkling. She bent at the waist, peering at the vibrating blade.

"I find it pleasant to hear!" Hyeon Won-chang called through a mouthful of provisions. "Quite a bit of elegance!"

That was when the strangers appeared on the road.

"Looking at them, they seem to be attending the Dragon Phoenix Gathering. Truly arrogant."

"Making sword sounds in broad daylight? What could it be if not showing off meager martial arts?"

Young. A man and a woman. Extraordinary energy. Luxurious yellow silk martial uniforms.

Swords at their waists.

Yeon-shin stood first, before Hyeon Won-chang could open his mouth. He clasped his fists and bowed.

"I was rash. Your words deeply resonate."

Their postures changed. They returned the greeting identically -- reflexive behavior, disciplined conduct. Etiquette drilled into them since childhood.

"He readily admits fault," the youth said. "Not bad."

"Maybe because we showed energy waves," the woman replied. "He must have been scared."

Both arrogant. Both dignified. The bearing of martial nobles.

"Your name and sect?"

Yeon-shin gave the false name. "Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect."

"Chasing Truth Sect?" The woman's lip curled. "Never heard of it. Someone without roots."

"Right," the youth said, thick eyebrows twitching. "We are bloodline of the Hwangbo family. Since I know all orthodox sects attending the Gathering, if your sect falls between righteous and evil, I cannot let you pass nicely."

*Hwangbo.*

The appearance description from Ma Jin's briefing surfaced in his memory. The face resembled it.

He felt Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin's energy strengthening behind him.

*It could end quickly.*

No need for patience. This was a mission requiring bloodshed in the orthodox realm. But justification came first.

He lowered his clasped hands.

"To speak of roots," Yeon-shin said. He let his original temperament show without concealment. "Though the Hwangbo family's prestige is high, your attitude is rude after hearing my sect's name. Is that martial world courtesy? Your insult scratched my sect's honor. How could a martial artist talk like that?"

"What...?"

"I first made courtesy and apologized. I revealed my sect and name even facing your unreasonable conduct. You speak of lacking roots while mentioning the Hwangbo family? Could you endure if you were me? I request sparring."

"Words flow like mountain streams."

The youth's mouth corners curled. He pushed the frowning woman back with his arm. Blood vessels protruded on his forearm -- tremendously refined blood energy.

"Good. My title is--"

"Not curious. Come."

Yeon-shin gripped Northern Light's hilt.

---

He did not draw the blade.

*I cannot kill here. The Namgung family's territory. Too many watching eyes.*

The youth's sword cleared its scabbard. A dazzling blade rising from his hip, upper body tilted forward -- an extremely narrow oval arc extending straight out.

Yeon-shin raised his right hand.

*Clang!*

He deflected the blade's flat with the back of his hand. The watching martial artists' eyes went wide. The onlookers who had gathered on the road stared in open disbelief.

The impact was light. His Hwan Yeok-bo step had already carried him to the diagonal, dispersing the force wave completely.

"What--!"

Yeon-shin advanced.

*Pung!*

His left arm extended with Fate Defying True Scripture amplification. Eternal Blossom Fist's force burst against the sword the youth reflexively raised, driving it upward. Gap opened.

True energy blazed through every meridian. The kidney transport point at his right waist ignited. Instantly his lower back and core muscles contained raw power. The linked combination was instantaneous.

He twisted his body. Extended his right fist.

The sensation of Hwangbo silk wrapping around his knuckles, smooth and expensive.

Then Eternal Blossom Fist's Advancing Thunder detonated.

*Bang!*

The youth's body flew like a stone from a sling.

Yeon-shin spread his right fingers and looked down. The moves were clear. Radiant Demon techniques melting into something uniquely his.

"The Hwangbo family's martial arts seem deep," he said. His voice was calm. Disappointment mixed in.

"But you are nothing special."

The face matched the appearance description. But this youth could not be the young master -- not if the rumors about Solar Divine Meridian and Namgung Hwa-sin refusing to spar were true.

"Probably not the young master. A second son?"

Silence. The growing crowd of onlookers had nothing to say.

The woman -- Hwangbo Myeong-rin, she named herself, her voice shaking with cold fury -- knelt beside her fallen brother, feeding him medicine.

"You... I will remember. Gu Gwa of the Chasing Truth Sect. I will not forget."

Yeon-shin had already moved. Through the attendants who scrambled to form a wall around the siblings -- piercing through them with body technique like wind through dry grass.

He stood over Hwangbo Myeong-rin.

"I request sparring with you too. You were the one who spoke of roots."

He gripped her nape and pulled her upright.

That was when the voice came from the side.

"Wait. Stop there."

A youth holding a flask. Wine smell rolling off him in waves. Perhaps ten years older than Yeon-shin. Silk martial clothes, but worn. Handsome face wasted to shadow. An opium poppy clenched between his teeth. Eyes ringed in black, heavy-lidded, and somehow amused.

The smile was languid.

"Though my siblings spoke wrongly," he said, "that is a bit harsh."

And then the heat hit.

Blazing yang energy burned from his entire body. A presence like standing too close to a furnace door -- tremendous, scorching, barely contained.

*Solar Divine Meridian.*

The appearance description matched perfectly.

This was the Hwangbo family's young master.

---
